methylTracer 0.99.8
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Cluster cells in a methylTracer object

Source: R/cluster_met.R
cluster_met_cells.Rd

Perform clustering of cells based on HDF5-backed region-level methylation values stored in a methylTracer object. The function implements a standard workflow:

  1. Select highly variable features.

  2. Perform PCA on the selected features.

  3. Run k-means clustering in PCA space and write cluster labels to /obs/<cluster_colname>.

  4. Optionally compute a 2D UMAP embedding and save it under /uns/umap_coords.

  5. Optionally build a k-nearest neighbor graph from the UMAP embedding and perform Leiden community detection, writing labels to /obs/<leiden_colname>.

The function can either require the input matrix to be free of missing values, or it can perform a simple feature-wise mean imputation internally. For single-cell methylation data, it is generally recommended to run compute_qc_value(), filter_obs_var() and impute_met_obj() before clustering, and use na_method = "error" here as a safeguard.

cluster_met_cells(
  met,
  n_clusters = 2,
  top_features = 2000,
  n_pcs = 20,
  cluster_colname = "cluster",
  seed = 1,
  save_pca = TRUE,
  pca_dataset = "uns/pca_coords",
  run_umap = TRUE,
  umap_n_neighbors = 3,
  umap_min_dist = 0.1,
  umap_dataset = "uns/umap_coords",
  run_leiden = TRUE,
  leiden_resolution = 0.5,
  leiden_colname = "leiden",
  na_method = c("error", "feature_mean"),
  max_na_frac = 0.5
)

Arguments

met

A methylTracer object with an on-disk methylation matrix stored under dataset "X".

n_clusters

Integer scalar; number of k-means clusters in PCA space.

top_features

Integer scalar; number of features (rows of X) with the highest variance (across cells) to use for PCA and clustering.

n_pcs

Integer scalar; number of principal components to retain from prcomp and use for k-means, UMAP and Leiden.

cluster_colname

Character scalar; name of the column written to /obs/<cluster_colname> with k-means cluster labels. Defaults to "cluster".

seed

Integer scalar; random seed used for PCA initialization, k-means, UMAP and Leiden (via underlying stochastic routines).

save_pca

Logical; if TRUE, PCA coordinates are written to /uns/pca_coords (or the dataset specified by pca_dataset).

pca_dataset

Character scalar; dataset path under /uns where PCA coordinates are stored. Defaults to "uns/pca_coords".

run_umap

Logical; if TRUE, compute a 2D UMAP embedding from the retained PCs.

umap_n_neighbors

Integer scalar; number of neighbors for UMAP. Larger values emphasize global structure at the cost of local detail.

umap_min_dist

Numeric scalar; UMAP min_dist parameter controlling how tightly points are allowed to pack in the embedding space.

umap_dataset

Character scalar; dataset path under /uns where UMAP coordinates are stored. Defaults to "uns/umap_coords".

run_leiden

Logical; if TRUE, construct a k-nearest neighbor graph from the UMAP nearest neighbor output and perform Leiden clustering.

leiden_resolution

Numeric scalar; resolution parameter passed to cluster_leiden. Higher values typically yield more fine-grained clusters.

leiden_colname

Character scalar; name of the column written to /obs/<leiden_colname> with Leiden cluster labels. Defaults to "leiden".

na_method

Character scalar; how to handle missing values in the selected feature matrix. One of:

  • "error": stop with an error if any NA is present.

  • "feature_mean": drop features with NA fraction greater than max_na_frac and mean-impute remaining NA values per feature (column).

max_na_frac

Numeric scalar in \([0,1]\); when na_method = "feature_mean", features with a fraction of NA values greater than max_na_frac are excluded before PCA. Defaults to 0.5.

Value

A named list with components:

  • cluster: character vector of k-means cluster labels (length = number of cells).

  • pca: data.frame of PCA coordinates (cells x PCs).

  • umap: data.frame of UMAP coordinates (cells x 2), or NULL if run_umap = FALSE.

  • leiden: character vector of Leiden cluster labels, or NULL if run_leiden = FALSE.

Side effects include writing cluster labels and embeddings into the HDF5 file associated with met.

Details

The function operates directly on the HDF5-backed matrix X via HDF5Array and DelayedMatrixStats. Variance-based feature selection is used to restrict computation to top_features rows. PCA is then performed on the (possibly imputed) in-memory matrix of shape cells x features.

UMAP is computed using uwot on the retained PCs. When run_leiden = TRUE, the k-nearest neighbor information returned by UMAP (ret_nn = TRUE) is used to build an undirected graph (via igraph), on which Leiden community detection is performed.

K-means cluster labels ("C1", "C2", ...) are written to /obs/<cluster_colname>, and Leiden cluster labels ("L1", "L2", ...) are written to /obs/<leiden_colname> in the underlying HDF5 file. PCA and UMAP coordinates are stored under /uns if requested.

See also

compute_qc_value, filter_obs_var, impute_met_obj, umap, cluster_leiden

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Assuming 'met' is a methylTracer object that has been QC-filtered
# and imputed (e.g. via compute_qc_value(), filter_obs_var(), impute_met_obj()).

clust_res <- cluster_met_cells(
  met,
  n_clusters       = 10,
  top_features     = 2000,
  n_pcs            = 30,
  run_umap         = TRUE,
  umap_n_neighbors = 30,
  umap_min_dist    = 0.3,
  run_leiden       = TRUE,
  leiden_resolution = 1.0,
  na_method        = "error"
)

table(clust_res$cluster)
table(clust_res$leiden)
} # }
